title: [FIRRTL] PlusArgsValue Intrinsic Creates Uninitialized Register
body:
Consider the following. The register `_pargs_v` is benignly uninitialized. This trips a lint warning:

```
FIRRTL version 3.1.0
circuit Foo :
  intmodule PlusArgsValueIntrinsic :
    output found : UInt<1>
    output result : UInt<32>
    intrinsic = circt_plusargs_value
    parameter FORMAT = "bar"

  module Foo :
    input clock : Clock
    input reset : UInt<1>
    output a : UInt<32> 

    inst PlusArgsValueIntrinsic of PlusArgsValueIntrinsic 
    node _a_T = mux(PlusArgsValueIntrinsic.found, PlusArgsValueIntrinsic.result, UInt<6>(0h20)) 
    connect a, _a_T 
```

This produces the following Verilog:
```
// Generated by CIRCT firtool-1.56.1-147-g8765933dc
module Foo(
  input         clock,
                reset,
  output [31:0] a
);

  reg [31:0] _pargs_v_;
  reg        _pargs_f;
  `ifdef SYNTHESIS
    assign _pargs_f = 1'h0;
  `else  // SYNTHESIS
    initial
      _pargs_f = |$value$plusargs("bar", _pargs_v_);
  `endif // SYNTHESIS
  assign a = _pargs_f ? _pargs_v_ : 32'h20;
endmodule
```
